_meta.source: Bedrock Edition data values + MakeCode pxt-minecraft constant naming conventions
_meta.naming_rule: Bedrock snake_case ID → UPPER_SNAKE_CASE in MakeCode Python. Use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE constants directly in code, never string literals.
_meta.note: Block IDs above 255 are Education Edition or newer additions. Some constants have naming exceptions documented per entry.
_meta.usage: blocks.place(GRASS, pos(0,0,0))  |  blocks.fill(STONE, pos(0,0,0), pos(5,5,5), FillOperation.REPLACE)

wood_and_logs.notes: blockWithData(LOG, 0) for oak; data 0-3 = oak/spruce/birch/jungle, upright

wool_and_dyed.notes: Default is white. Use blockWithData(WOOL, n) for colours

special_and_education.notes: Education Edition — allows building in region
Education Edition — prevents building in region

items._note: Items used with mobs.give(), agent.setItem(), etc. Item IDs are > 255 in Bedrock.

mobs._note: Mob constants used with mobs.spawn(), mobs.kill(), mobs.onMobKilled() etc.

special.notes: Not a living entity — summons lightning at position
Education Edition only — the programmable agent

selectors._note: Selector constants used with mobs.target(), gameplay.setGameMode() etc.

values.description: Closest player to the event origin
All living entities in the world
